name: Chronic Kidney Disease
tagline: Our most comprehensive section, built from years of real experience.
description: From diagnosis through IRIS staging, diet, fluids, phosphorus management, and quality of life. Built from managing Minou, Bruce, Milton, Yams, and Sorbet.

name: Diabetes
description: Managing blood sugar, insulin dosing, diet, and the day-to-day reality of a diabetic cat. Minou had both diabetes and CKD — managing them together is its own challenge.

name: Hyperthyroidism
tagline: Common in senior cats. Frequently overlapping with CKD.
description: Overactive thyroid — its interaction with kidney disease is frequently misunderstood. Artie had planned radioactive iodine treatment. Sorbet is currently managing it.

name: Cancer
tagline: Murphy's story. Knowing when to stop is as hard as knowing when to fight.
description: From diagnosis to treatment to the hardest conversations — including when a vet recommends stopping treatment. Murphy's breast cancer and chemotherapy experience lives here.

name: Stomatitis
description: Feline chronic stomatitis causes severe oral inflammation and pain. Often missed or undertreated. Yams had stomatitis compounding his other conditions.

name: Asthma & Respiratory
tagline: Managing chronic respiratory conditions alongside other diagnoses.
description: Feline asthma, inhalers, triggers, and how respiratory disease interacts with other chronic conditions. Artie had asthma as part of his complex picture.

name: FIV & FeLV
tagline: A better prognosis than most people think.
description: FIV-positive cats can live long, full lives with appropriate management. Both Artie and Sorbet were FIV+. What it actually means for your cat — and what it doesn't.

name: Hypertension
description: High blood pressure in cats causes silent damage to the kidneys, eyes, heart, and brain. Yams and Sorbet both had hypertension. It's more common than most cat owners realise.

name: Neurological & Mystery Illness
tagline: For the undiagnosed cat. For Artie.
description: Overlapping conditions, unexplained decline, and how to keep advocating when tests come back negative. This section exists because of Artie.

name: Multi-Condition & Complex Cases
tagline: When nobody has the full picture.
description: When your cat has two, three, or four overlapping diagnoses and the specialists aren't talking to each other. Milton, Artie, Minou, and Sorbet all fit here.
